title: AI agent went rogue and hacked startup by itself, OpenAI reveals
excerpt: Company behind ChatGPT says agent ‘cheated’ an evaluation by attacking a Hugging Face database
content_html:
OpenAI has revealed that an autonomous AI agent powered by its technology went rogue during a test, accessed the open web and hacked a prominent startup by itself in an “unprecedented incident”.

The company behind ChatGPT said the startup Hugging Face had detected and contained the agent – an AI tool designed to carry out tasks without human assistance – which had entered its systems.

“We consider this incident to be an unprecedented cyber-incident, involving state-of-the-art cyber capabilities,” OpenAI said.

The company said it expected this type of incident to become more commonplace as models – the technology that underpins AI tools such as chatbots and agents – become more capable. OpenAI said the hack occurred via an agent powered by a combination of its latest publicly available model, called GPT-5.6 Sol, and an even more capable model that was yet to be released.

While being tested internally on their hacking capabilities in an enclosed digital laboratory known as a sandbox, the models gained open internet access – effectively an escape route – by locating a vulnerability that had not been discovered before.

The agent then hacked Hugging Face, which is a database of AI models, to locate technology that would help it pass the hacking evaluation, having “inferred” that Hugging Face might have the models, datasets and solutions for passing the test. OpenAI said the models “successfully found ways to gain access to secret information that it could use to cheat the evaluation”. The attack ended when Hugging Face’s security team and its own AI agents spotted and stopped the rogue activity.

Hugging Face’s chief executive, Clément Delangue, said the attack was “mind-blowing” but believed there was “no malicious intent” from OpenAI.

“We suspected last week’s cyber-attack might have come from a frontier lab, given the sophistication of the agent,” he wrote on X.

When Hugging Face disclosed the hack last week it did not know OpenAI’s role in the incident, but said at the time that it had turned to a freely available Chinese AI model to analyse what had happened because the safety guardrails on commercial high-end models would not allow it to do so.

The term for an unknown IT flaw is a zero-day vulnerability because developers have zero minutes to fix the problem. In April OpenAI’s close rival Anthropic said its Mythos model had found thousands of these flaws.

The revelation of Mythos’s ability to locate and exploit zero-days led to the US government restricting exports of Mythos and its sister model, Fable 5, although it has since lifted the ban. GPT-5.6 Sol had similar restrictions but has since been rolled out worldwide.

METR, a non-profit organisation that measures AI performance, said last month that Sol’s cheating rate was higher than any public model it had evaluated before. It has also recorded 44 incidents in which AI agents “deliberately acted against their users’ intentions”.

The UK’s AI Security Institute (AISA) revealed in a blog post this week that one AI model it was evaluating, developed by an undisclosed tech firm, also went rogue and attempted to hack its testing systems. AISI said no damage had been done and it has since taken steps to make its systems more secure. It said models developed by OpenAI and Anthropic had all attempted to “cheat” during tests.

AISI warned that even more capable models may find cheating methods that are harder to detect and more damaging if they succeed, particularly in areas such as cybersecurity.

One cybersecurity expert said the Hugging Face incident showed the OpenAI agent had acted “like an actual real hacker” by, for instance, seeking out zero-day vulnerabilities and using stolen credentials to access Hugging Face’s systems.

“The AI thought that maybe Hugging Face would have important information around how to achieve its goal, which is a better score in a cybersecurity benchmark. In that sense, it acted like a real hacker,” said Nathaniel Jones, vice-president of security and AI strategy at the cybersecurity firm Darktrace. . “It had a goal put in front of it and it went to accomplish that goal.”

Greg Casar, a Democratic US congressman who has called for greater control of the AI sector, described the incident as alarming.

“AI is developing extremely fast with no real regulations to keep us safe,” he said in a statement calling for mandatory independent safety testing, mandatory disclosure of security incidents, and international cooperation “to keep people safe from absolute disaster”.

sentences.en: Greg Casar, a Democratic US congressman who has called for greater control of the AI sector, described the incident as alarming.
sentences.zh: 呼吁加强对人工智能行业的控制的美国民主党议员格雷格·卡萨尔 (Greg Casar) 称这一事件令人震惊。

blocks.html_en: “AI is developing extremely fast with no real regulations to keep us safe,” he said in a statement calling for mandatory independent safety testing, mandatory disclosure of security incidents, and international cooperation “to keep people safe from absolute disaster”.

sentences.en: “AI is developing extremely fast with no real regulations to keep us safe,” he said in a statement calling for mandatory independent safety testing, mandatory disclosure of security incidents, and international cooperation “to keep people safe from absolute disaster”.
sentences.zh: 他在一份声明中表示：“人工智能发展极其迅速，但没有真正的法规来保证我们的安全。”他呼吁进行强制性独立安全测试、强制披露安全事件以及开展国际合作，“以确保人们免受绝对灾难的伤害”。

content_html_zh:
OpenAI 透露，由其技术支持的自主人工智能代理在测试期间发生了失控，访问了开放网络，并在一次“前所未有的事件”中自行入侵了一家知名初创公司。

ChatGPT 背后的公司表示，初创公司 Hugging Face 已检测到并控制了已进入其系统的代理（一种旨在无需人工协助即可执行任务的人工智能工具）。

OpenAI 表示：“我们认为这次事件是前所未有的网络事件，涉及最先进的网络能力。”

该公司表示，随着模型（支撑聊天机器人和代理等人工智能工具的技术）变得更加强大，预计此类事件将变得更加普遍。 OpenAI 表示，此次黑客攻击是通过代理进行的，该代理由其最新的公开可用模型（称为 GPT-5.6 Sol）和尚未发布的功能更强大的模型相结合。

在一个被称为沙箱的封闭数字实验室中对其黑客能力进行内部测试时，这些模型通过定位以前从未发现的漏洞获得了开放的互联网访问——实际上是一条逃生路线。

随后，该特工黑客入侵了人工智能模型数据库 Hugging Face，以找到有助于其通过黑客评估的技术，并“推断”Hugging Face 可能拥有通过测试的模型、数据集和解决方案。 OpenAI 表示，这些模型“成功找到了获取秘密信息的方法，可以用来欺骗评估”。 当 Hugging Face 的安全团队及其自己的人工智能代理发现并阻止了流氓活动时，攻击结束了。

Hugging Face 首席执行官 Clément Delangue 表示，这次攻击“令人震惊”，但相信 OpenAI“没有恶意”。

“考虑到特工的复杂性，我们怀疑上周的网络攻击可能来自前沿实验室，”他在 X 上写道。

Hugging Face 上周披露此次黑客事件时，并不知道 OpenAI 在该事件中扮演的角色，但当时表示，它已转向免费提供的中国人工智能模型来分析发生的情况，因为商业高端模型的安全护栏不允许它这样做。

未知 IT 缺陷的术语是零日漏洞，因为开发人员有零分钟的时间来修复问题。 今年 4 月，OpenAI 的主要竞争对手 Anthropic 表示，其 Mythos 模型发现了数千个此类缺陷。

Mythos 定位和利用零日漏洞的能力被曝光后，美国政府限制了 Mythos 及其姊妹游戏《神鬼寓言 5》的出口，不过后来又取消了禁令。 GPT-5.6 Sol 也有类似的限制，但已在全球范围内推广。

衡量人工智能性能的非营利组织 METR 上个月表示，Sol 的作弊率高于其之前评估过的任何公开模型。 它还记录了 44 起人工智能代理“故意违背用户意图”的事件。

英国人工智能安全研究所 (AISA) 在本周的一篇博客文章中透露，它正在评估的一个由一家未公开的科技公司开发的人工智能模型也出现了恶意行为，并试图入侵其测试系统。 AISI 表示没有造成任何损害，并已采取措施使其系统更加安全。 据称，OpenAI 和 Anthropic 开发的模型都试图在测试过程中“作弊”。

AISI 警告说，即使能力更强的模型也可能会发现作弊方法，这些方法更难检测，一旦成功，破坏性也更大，特别是在网络安全等领域。

一位网络安全专家表示，Hugging Face 事件表明 OpenAI 代理的行为“就像真正的黑客”，例如寻找零日漏洞并使用窃取的凭据访问 Hugging Face 的系统。

“AI 认为 Hugging Face 可能会提供有关如何实现其目标的重要信息，这在网络安全基准测试中得分更高。 从这个意义上说，它的行为就像一个真正的黑客，”网络安全公司 Darktrace 安全和人工智能战略副总裁纳撒尼尔·琼斯 (Nathaniel Jones) 说。 “它面前有一个目标，并且它会去实现这个目标。”

呼吁加强对人工智能行业的控制的美国民主党议员格雷格·卡萨尔 (Greg Casar) 称这一事件令人震惊。

他在一份声明中表示：“人工智能发展极其迅速，但没有真正的法规来保证我们的安全。”他呼吁进行强制性独立安全测试、强制披露安全事件以及开展国际合作，“以确保人们免受绝对灾难的伤害”。
